symptoms.symptom_name: Rash onset 48 h after new, localised metal (belt-buckle) skin contact, confined to the contact site

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A delayed (24–72 h) eruption mapped exactly to a new contact allergen is the defining temporal-spatial signature of allergic contact dermatitis.
Zoster follows a dermatome and is not tied to an external contact object.
Pruritus is the dominant symptom of a spongiotic contact dermatitis.
Zoster is characteristically painful/neuropathic rather than predominantly itchy.
Impetigo is more often mildly sore/tender than intensely pruritic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sharply demarcated geometric (object-shaped) margins with no dermatomal or midline-band distribution and no spread beyond the contact site

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unnaturally straight/geometric borders matching an external object are near-specific for a contact reaction.
A non-dermatomal, non-band distribution excludes zoster.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vesicles weeping clear serous fluid with light crusting (not honey-coloured purulent crust)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vesiculation with clear serous exudate is typical of an acute spongiotic dermatitis.
Impetigo classically produces thick honey-coloured purulent crust, not clear serous weeping.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no generalised urticaria, no angioedema, no respiratory symptoms — systemically well

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of any systemic or airway features excludes an IgE-mediated systemic reaction and keeps this a localised, low-tier problem.
No fever or spreading warmth argues against secondary bacterial cellulitis at present.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior mild skin irritation from cheap metal jewellery/watch backs (suggests background nickel sensitisation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A history of reacting to cheap metal items is consistent with pre-existing nickel sensitisation, the likely allergen here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive epicutaneous patch testing to the causative allergen (e.g. nickel)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patch testing confirms the specific allergen and is the gold standard, but it is a dermatology outpatient test, not available in the acute telehealth encounter and not needed to manage this episode.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: New localised metal contact + intense pruritus + geometric object-shaped margins together are near-diagnostic of allergic contact dermatitis and simultaneously exclude the vesicular mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not acutely indicated; appropriate only if the dermatitis is recurrent or the allergen is unclear, to confirm the specific sensitiser. Ordering it acutely would be over-investigation.
Not indicated now — the exudate is clear serous fluid with no purulence. Reserve for later if honey-coloured crusting or purulent discharge develops (see RF-003).
